name: Mars Express
first: Europe's first interplanetary mission; longest-running non-NASA Mars orbiter
description: ESA's first mission to a planet other than Earth. The orbiter has produced the longest continuous record of Mars's atmosphere, mapping water ice deposits, methane plumes, and the planet's three-dimensional weather. Beagle 2, the British-built lander it carried, reached the surface but never deployed solar panels fully — declared lost in 2004, found intact by NASA's Mars Reconnaissance Orbiter in 2015. The orbiter is in its third decade of operation.

events.label: BEAGLE 2 RELEASE
events.note: Lander separates from orbiter for independent descent.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: 37-minute main engine burn captures orbiter into Mars orbit on Christmas Day 2003.

events.label: BEAGLE 2 SILENT
events.note: Expected first signal not received; lander declared lost.
